strategy_criteria_summary: USA-listed companies reporting earnings next week, sorted by market cap. This deliberately broad Finviz screen preserves the top five visible rows; optionability, liquidity, trend, news, fundamentals, and contract quality are validated downstream.
scoring_reasons: trend alignment was clearly supportive|market/sector environment was clearly supportive|price structure was clearly supportive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-15 97.5
decision_reasoning: IONQ has the most decisive directional setup in the slate: +80% over 20d, +51% over 50d, and relative strength of +72% vs SPY and +59% vs sector. The 51-strike 2026-05-08 call is near-the-money with the best liquidity in the candidate chain (volume 925, spread ~6%). However, IV at 152% signals very elevated earnings premium and material post-print IV crush risk, news flow is sparse (no confirming catalyst), and the expiry sits only one day past earnings, so a muted move destroys the position. SWKS news is mixed-to-bearish on guidance, CSCO's 97.5 strike is far OTM requiring a ~7% move with negative sector RS, and CRWV's only listed strike ($200 vs $131 spot) is not a credible setup. IONQ is the best single idea but does not clear the recommend bar — monitor for an entry with tempered IV or a pullback toward the 20-day.
